steps.title: Identify the contrast signal
steps.content: The passage contrasts two causes of agglomeration: knowledge spillovers drive clustering in one context, but different reasons drive it in another. This signals that the mix of factors driving agglomeration varies by industry.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the blank
steps.content:
The mix of factors driving agglomeration is ______ across industries — the passage contrasts knowledge spillovers (tech clusters) with different reasons (tube manufacturers), so the blank must mean varied or differing by industry. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — credible: believable or trustworthy — the passage is about what varies, not what's believable. ✗
  • Choice B — heterogeneous: varied or differing in kind — exactly captures a mix of factors that differs across industries. ✓
  • Choice C — imperative: urgently necessary — the passage doesn't argue these factors are urgent. ✗
  • Choice D — decisive: conclusive or final — describes strength of influence, not variation across industries. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
